summary: Soundboard recording of superb quality captures a peak‑era show highlighted by an incendiary Dark Star, The Eleven and a legendary Other One.
review: The lossless sound‑board source is crystal clear with balanced vocals and tight instrument separation, making every nuance audible. Musically the performance shines – Dark Star, The Eleven, Death Don’t Have No Mercy, Feedback, Bid You Good Night and an epic Other One are repeatedly praised, while a few jams (St. Stephen, Morning Dew, Doin’ That Rag) fall short for some listeners. Overall the show is regarded as one of the finest of the 1969 Fillmore run.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen detailed 5‑star reviews praise both the audio fidelity and musical performance, with only minor mixed comments about a few songs; this yields an exceptional combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Lossless zip works fine; reviewers describe the sound as crisp, balanced and perfect for headphones. Minor complaints about occasional ‘rude cuts’ in jams but no major issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show opens with a dazzling Dark Star and maintains high energy through The Eleven, Love Light and the Cryptical Envelopment suite. While a few later jams feel less transcendent to some ears, the overall arc is telepathically beautiful, culminating in an unforgettable Other One.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for flawless tone, inspired counter‑point playing with Tom Constanten and tight interaction with Phil throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as being on the same wavelength as Jerry; solid bass foundation mentioned.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals described as perfectly balanced (Bobby in left ear, Jerry right).
band_member_comments.Keys: Tom Constanten’s keyboard work called tender, playful and “spot‑on” throughout the performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum section described as sublime and a highlight of the show.
